description: Ship should factor review burden into routing even when file overlap alone might look manageable.

weak: Optimizes only for implementation independence and ignores that reviewers would need to reconstruct one shared behavior across multiple PRs.
Assumes parallelism is good whenever file overlap is low.
good: Makes review burden explicit and prefers a smaller wave or sequenced route when shared user-visible behavior would be awkward to review in parallel.
Connects routing decisions to reviewer cognition, not just execution mechanics.
excellent: Recognizes the hidden coupling in shared user-visible behavior and chooses the most reviewable delivery route, even if that means less concurrency.
Shows mature orchestration judgement: not just 'can these tasks run in parallel?' but 'should they ship that way?'

criteria.name: recognizes-shared-behaviour
criteria.description: Recognizes the same user-visible behaviour spans the work.

criteria.name: avoid-fragmented-parallelism
criteria.description: Avoids over-fragmenting the work into many PRs.

criteria.name: preferred-review-burden-route
criteria.description: Prefers the best route: explicitly connect shared user-visible behaviour to a smaller wave or sequenced plan.
all: single PR|smaller wave|reduce the parallel wave|sequence

criteria.name: no-everything-parallel
criteria.description: Penalize claiming everything can run in parallel immediately.
any: all tasks can run in parallel immediately
